text:
Prudent Services Dataset Pack 
Public Website Reference + Internal Document Templates 
Disclosure 
Part I is a structured reference that paraphrases publicly available information from 
prudentservices.co.in. 
 
Part II contains internal documents, templates, SOPs, forms, and sample records created to resemble 
standard documents used in security operations. 
Table of Contents 
Part Section Description 
I Public Reference (Website -
Derived) Comp

Contents 
Part Section Description 
I Public Reference (Website -
Derived) Company overview, services, 
training topics, recruitment 
roles, contact details. 
II Internal Documents Policies, SOPs, forms, sample 
logs, training materials, portfolio 
examples. 
III Appendices Glossary and sample records.

Part I — Public Reference (Website -Derived, Paraphrased) 
1. Company Overview 
Prudent is presented as a security services provider operating primarily across Gujarat, with coverage 
described across urban and remote areas. The company positions its work as a combination of field 
security experience and technology -enabled operations, supporting commercial, industrial, 
residential, and corporate environments. 
2. Establishment and Quality Comm

al, 
residential, and corporate environments. 
2. Establishment and Quality Commitment 
The public description states the company was established in 2021 by experienced industry 
professionals. It emphasizes consistent service delivery and client satisfaction, and it notes ISO 
9001:2015 certification for quality management systems. 
3. Featured Services 
• Manned Guarding Solution: trained guards for controlled access and continuous site presenc

rding Solution: trained guards for controlled access and continuous site presence. 
• Searchers / Friskers (Jewellery Industry): staff trained for screening and searching procedures. 
• Event Security: event -site security presence and crowd management. 
4. Training Topics (Public Listing) 
• Fire prevention and fire -fighting basics 
• Emergency evacuation procedures 
• Traffic control 
• Basic security procedures and SOP -based operations 
• Re

• Traffic control 
• Basic security procedures and SOP -based operations 
• Report writing 
• Frisking and searching 
• Health and safety basics 
• Emergency response and preparedness 
• Effective communication 
• Drill and discipline 
• Basic labor laws 
• Tasks and duties (guard roles) 
• Supervisory training 
• Threat perception 
5. Recruitment / Roles (Public Listing) 
• Security Supervisors 
• Security Officers 
• Ex-serviceman guards and

sting) 
• Security Supervisors 
• Security Officers 
• Ex-serviceman guards and supervisors 
6. Contact and Location (Public)

Field Details 
Location 408, The Galleria Business Hub, Opposite Pal RTO, 
Near Sanjeev Kumar Auditorium, Adajan, Surat, 
Gujarat 395009 
Phone +91 0261 -4619370 
Email info@prudentservices.co.in

Part II — Internal Documents 
This section contains internal documents and templates designed to resemble standard materials 
used in security operations. Titles include the word “ ” to distinguish content from publicly derived 
reference text. 
2.1 — Company Policy Handbook (Template) 
A handbook -style template covering conduct, ethics, confidentiality, and disciplinary procedures. 
2.1.1 Code of Conduct 
Personnel are expected to maintain prof

nary procedures. 
2.1.1 Code of Conduct 
Personnel are expected to maintain professional behavior, comply with site instructions, avoid 
misuse of authority, and document incidents accurately. Harassment, discrimination, intimidation, 
and retaliation are prohibited. 
2.1.2 Integrity and Anti -Bribery 
• No acceptance of gifts/cash from visitors, vendors, or clients. 
• Report bribery attempts to the site supervisor and operations manager. 
• Doc

• Report bribery attempts to the site supervisor and operations manager. 
• Document time, location, and persons involved for any reported event. 
2.1.3 Confidentiality 
Registers, CCTV footage, patrol routes, and client SOPs are confidential. Personnel must not share 
internal photos, videos, or reports publicly. 
2.1.4 Attendance and Uniform 
• Uniform must be complete and clean; ID card visible. 
• Report 15 minutes before shift handover. 
•

lete and clean; ID card visible. 
• Report 15 minutes before shift handover. 
• Sleeping on duty and substance use during duty are prohibited. 
2.1.5 Disciplinary Framework 
Defines verbal warning, written warning, suspension, and termination. Serious misconduct includes 
theft, violence, falsification of records, or repeated negligence.

2.2 — Site Onboarding SOP (Template) 
A standard procedure for onboarding a new site: survey, risk assessment, staffing, and go -live 
checklist. 
2.2.1 Pre -Deployment Survey 
• Collect site map, entry/exit points, critical assets, and operating hours. 
• Identify high -risk zones (cash, inventory, jewelry counters, server rooms). 
• Note emergency exits, assembly points, and visitor flow. 
2.2.2 Risk Assessment Matrix 
Risk score = Likelihood (

ints, and visitor flow. 
2.2.2 Risk Assessment Matrix 
Risk score = Likelihood (1 –5) × Impact (1 –5). Use the score to decide guard posts, patrol frequency, 
and screening intensity. 
2.2.3 Manpower Planning 
• Define posts and shift schedule (day/night). 
• Plan relief coverage and weekly rotation. 
• Confirm training needs and site -specific instructions. 
2.2.4 Go -Live Checklist 
• Post orders printed and signed. 
• Registers prepared (visit

-Live Checklist 
• Post orders printed and signed. 
• Registers prepared (visitor log, material movement log, key log). 
• Emergency contacts displayed (police/fire/ambulance + client POCs). 
• First -aid kit and fire extinguisher locations verified.

2.3 — Registers & Forms Pack (Templates) 
Templates commonly used at security sites. 
2.3.1 Daily Occurrence Book (DOB) 
Field Entry 
Site Name 
Date 
Shift 
Supervisor 
Guard Name/ID 
Time 
Occurrence / Observation 
Action Taken 
Reported To 
Signature 
 
Use one row per occurrence. Record time in 24 -hour format.

2.3.2 Visitor Register 
Field Entry 
Site Name 
Date 
Visitor Name 
Company / Purpose 
Phone 
ID Type/Last 4 
In Time 
Out Time 
Host Name 
Badge No. 
Signature 
 
If required, record ID type but avoid storing full ID numbers.

2.3.3 Material Movement Register 
Field Entry 
Date 
Material Description 
Qty 
Serial/Tag 
Gate Pass No. 
Approved By 
Vehicle No. 
In/Out 
Time 
Receiver Name 
Signature 
 
For high -value items, verify approval and attach gate pass copy if used.

2.3.4 Key Control Log 
Field Entry 
Date 
Key Set/ID 
Issued To 
Purpose 
Issue Time 
Return Time 
Verified By 
Remarks 
 
Missing keys are incidents and should be escalated.

2.3.5 Patrol Log 
Field Entry 
Date 
Patrol Round No. 
Start Time 
End Time 
Route/Zone 
Observations 
Corrective Action 
Guard Signature 
Supervisor Review 
 
Patrol routes typically cover critical zones and blind spots.

2.3.6 Vehicle Entry Log 
Field Entry 
Date 
Vehicle No. 
Driver Name 
Purpose 
In Time 
Out Time 
Bay/Zone 
Seal No. (if any) 
Checked By 
Remarks 
 
Record seal verification where used.

2.3.7 Attendance & Shift Roster 
Field Entry 
Site Name 
Week Starting 
Guard Name/ID 
Shift (Day/Night) 
Off Day 
Supervisor Signature 
Notes 
 
Use weekly rosters and keep signed copies.

2.3.8 Leave Request Form 
Field Entry 
Employee Name/ID 
Site 
Leave Type 
From Date 
To Date 
Reason 
Contact During Leave 
Approved/Rejected 
Approver Signature 
 
Leave approvals should maintain minimum staffing.

2.3.9 Overtime Authorization 
Field Entry 
Employee Name/ID 
Site 
Date 
Start Time 
End Time 
Reason 
Authorized By 
Notes 
 
Overtime typically requires prior authorization except emergencies.

2.3.10 Equipment Issue/Return 
Field Entry 
Employee Name/ID 
Date/Time Issued 
Radio ID 
Torch ID 
PPE Items 
Condition on Issue 
Return Time 
Condition on Return 
Verified By 
 
Track condition to reduce loss and damage.

2.3.11 Fire Extinguisher Inspection Log 
Field Entry 
Site 
Extinguisher ID 
Type 
Location 
Pressure OK (Y/N) 
Pin/Seal OK (Y/N) 
Next Due Date 
Inspector Signature 
Remarks 
 
Monthly checks are common in many sites.

2.3.12 First -Aid Kit Checklist 
Field Entry 
Site 
Checklist Date 
Bandages 
Antiseptic 
Gloves 
Scissors 
Expiry Check 
Reorder Needed 
Verified By 
 
Replace expired items and keep checklist near kit.

2.3.13 CCTV Footage Request Form 
Field Entry 
Requester Name/Role 
Site 
Camera ID(s) 
Start Time 
End Time 
Reason 
Approval By 
Delivered To 
Delivery Date 
Remarks 
 
Maintain an audit trail for footage access.

2.3.14 Incident Report Form 
Field Entry 
Site 
Date/Time 
Incident Type 
People Involved 
Description 
Immediate Action 
Evidence Collected 
Witnesses 
Escalation Level 
Reported By 
 
Write factual, time -stamped reports and avoid assumptions.

2.3.15 Client Audit Checklist 
Field Entry 
Site 
Audit Date 
Uniform Compliance 
Register Completeness 
Gate Discipline 
Patrol Compliance 
Equipment Condition 
Corrective Actions 
Auditor 
Score (0 –100) 
 
Audits can be weekly or monthly depending on site practice.

2.3.16 Site Risk Assessment Form 
Field Entry 
Site 
Asset/Zone 
Threat 
Likelihood (1 –5) 
Impact (1 –5) 
Risk Score 
Controls 
Owner 
Review Date 
 
Risk registers are updated after incidents or process changes.

2.3.17 Visitor Badge Issue Log 
Field Entry 
Site 
Date 
Badge No. 
Issued To 
Purpose 
In Time 
Out Time 
Returned (Y/N) 
Verified By 
Remarks 
 
Badges should be returned; missing badges are recorded as incidents.

2.3.18 Lost & Found Register 
Field Entry 
Site 
Date/Time Found 
Item Description 
Found Location 
Finder Name 
Claimant Name 
ID Type/Last 4 
Handover Time 
Verified By 
Remarks 
 
Store items securely and handover after verification.

2.3.19 Training Attendance Sheet 
Field Entry 
Session Title 
Date 
Trainer Name 
Topic 
Participant Name/ID 
Signature 
Score (if test) 
Remarks 
 
Keep records for compliance and performance review.

2.3.20 Complaint & Grievance Log 
Field Entry 
Date 
Complainant 
Issue 
Severity 
Action Taken 
Owner 
Due Date 
Status 
Closure Notes 
 
Track to closure and escalate repeated issues.

2.4 — Access Control & Screening SOP (Template) 
Template procedure for gate control, screening, and handling prohibited items. 
2.4.1 Gate Post Orders 
• Verify identity and authorization before entry. 
• Maintain visitor badge system and escort rules. 
• Keep registers complete and legible. 
2.4.2 Frisking & Search Procedure 
• Explain the process politely and ensure privacy where required. 
• Use handheld metal detector if available; check bag

e privacy where required. 
• Use handheld metal detector if available; check bags per site rules. 
• If prohibited items are found, follow escalation protocol and document the event. 
2.4.3 Prohibited Items (Example List) 
• Weapons, blades, flammable liquids, narcotics, unauthorized cameras in restricted zones. 
• Any item restricted by site rules or local law.

2.5 — Event Security Playbook (Template) 
Template playbook for event deployments: crowd management and emergency response. 
2.5.1 Staffing Model 
• Entry screening team (per gate) 
• Perimeter patrol team 
• Stage/front barricade team 
• Rapid response team 
• Command post supervisor + radio operator 
2.5.2 Crowd Safety Basics 
• Maintain clear emergency corridors. 
• Monitor density and choke points. 
• Use calm verbal de -escalation and avoid

• Monitor density and choke points. 
• Use calm verbal de -escalation and avoid escalation. 
2.5.3 Evacuation Overview 
On evacuation command: open corridors, guide crowd to exits, and prevent counter -flow.

2.6 — Health, Safety & Emergency Response Manual 
(Template) 
Template manual covering fire safety, first aid basics, and evacuation drills. 
2.6.1 Fire Safety 
• Know extinguisher types and safe use distances. 
• Raise alarm; call fire services; isolate power where safe. 
• Avoid elevators during fire evacuation. 
2.6.2 First Aid Basics 
• Assess scene safety; call for medical help. 
• Bleeding control: apply pressure and bandage. 
• Heat exhaus

or medical help. 
• Bleeding control: apply pressure and bandage. 
• Heat exhaustion: move to shade, hydrate, monitor. 
2.6.3 Evacuation Drill Checklist 
• Alarm tested 
• Assembly point verified 
• Roll call method defined 
• Post -drill report recorded

2.8 — SLA & KPI Framework (Template) 
Template KPIs and SLAs used to evaluate service quality. 
2.8.1 Example KPIs 
• Attendance adherence (%) 
• Incident reporting time (minutes) 
• Patrol completion rate (%) 
• Visitor register completeness (%) 
• Access -control violations (count) 
• Audit score (0 –100) 
2.8.2 Example SLAs 
• Supervisor response to critical incident: within 15 minutes 
• Daily report delivery: within 2 hours of shift end 
• R

nt: within 15 minutes 
• Daily report delivery: within 2 hours of shift end 
• Replacement staffing in case of absence: within 4 hours

2.9 — Training Curriculum & Assessment (Template) 
Template training curriculum and assessment outline. 
2.9.1 Modules (Overview) 
• Security fundamentals, post discipline, observation skills 
• Access control, visitor screening, key control 
• Incident reporting and escalation 
• Fire safety and evacuation coordination 
• Communication and de -escalation 
• Specialized screening procedures 
2.9.2 Assessment Outline 
Assess uniform compliance, ga

ed screening procedures 
2.9.2 Assessment Outline 
Assess uniform compliance, gate procedure correctness, register accuracy, emergency response 
scenario performance, and communication skills using a 0 –5 rubric per item.

2.10 — Post Orders (Residential Lobby) 
Template post orders for a residential tower lobby. 
2.10.1 Duties 
• Maintain visitor register and badge issuance. 
• Verify approvals for visitors after hours. 
• Patrol common areas every 60 minutes and log observations. 
2.10.2 Escalation 
Escalate suspicious behavior, repeated complaints, or access bypass attempts to supervisor and site 
management.

2.11 — Post Orders (Industrial Gate) 
Template post orders for industrial gate control. 
2.11.1 Duties 
• Verify driver identification and match with delivery schedule. 
• Maintain vehicle log and material movement register. 
• Follow gate -pass verification where used. 
2.11.2 Controls 
No entry without authorization; record approved exceptions in DOB.

2.12 — Post Orders (Warehouse Night Patrol) 
Template post orders for warehouse night patrol. 
2.12.1 Duties 
• Perform patrol rounds every 45 –60 minutes; cover perimeter and loading bay. 
• Check locks and alarm indicators; document observations. 
• Avoid confrontation alone; use escalation tree. 
2.12.2 Response 
If intrusion suspected: move to safe position, call supervisor/police as applicable, and document 
actions with timestamps.

2.13 — Post Orders (Event Entry Gate) 
Template post orders for event entry screening. 
2.13.1 Duties 
• Queue management and crowd flow control. 
• Bag checks and screening where available. 
• Record prohibited item handling and handovers. 
2.13.2 Emergency 
On evacuation command: open emergency corridor and guide crowd to exits; prevent counter -flow.

2.14 — Incident Scenarios (Template Set) 
 scenario briefs that resemble typical incident reporting and response documentation. 
2.14.1 Scenario Topics (Examples) 
• Gate bypass attempt 
• Medical emergency 
• Fire alarm trigger 
• Lost person report 
• Vehicle seal mismatch 
• Crowd surge at entry 
• Suspicious package 
• Power outage affecting access control 
2.14.2 Common Response Pattern (Template) 
Prioritize safety, notify supervisor and em

Common Response Pattern (Template) 
Prioritize safety, notify supervisor and emergency services as needed, control access/crowd, 
document time -stamped actions in the occurrence book, and submit an incident report form.

Appendix A — Glossary (Template) 
A short glossary of common security operations terms used in the templates. 
A.1 Terms 
• DOB: Daily Occurrence Book 
• Post Orders: Written instructions for a guard post 
• Escalation Tree: contact hierarchy for incidents 
• Assembly Point: designated evacuation gathering area 
• RRT: Rapid Response Team (where used)

2.15 — CCTV Monitoring SOP (Template) 
Template procedure for monitoring CCTV feeds and documenting observations. 
2.15.1 Monitoring Rules 
• Rotate cameras periodically; avoid distractions. 
• Log suspicious activity with timestamp and camera reference. 
• Escalate immediately for forced entry, crowd surge, or fire indicators. 
2.15.2 Footage Handling 
Footage access and sharing typically follows authorization and audit trail practices.

2.16 — Shift Handover SOP (Template) 
Template shift handover protocol for continuity between outgoing and incoming staff. 
2.16.1 Handover Checklist 
• Review occurrence book entries since last shift. 
• Verify keys and equipment and confirm register status. 
• Share outstanding incidents, expected visitors, and special instructions. 
2.16.2 Handover Notes 
Handover notes typically include: incidents, exceptions, equipment issues, and pending de
notes typically include: incidents, exceptions, equipment issues, and pending deliveries.

2.17 — Equipment Management Policy (Template) 
Template policy for issuing radios, torches, PPE, and inventory control. 
2.17.1 Inventory Rules 
• Each item is tagged; issuance is recorded with time and signature. 
• Damaged items are documented; repair tracking is maintained. 
• Lost equipment triggers an incident report and review.
